title: hieracium umbellatum var. boreale
intro: Hieracium umbellatum var. boreale, also known as the northern hawk's-beard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hieracium umbellatum var. boreale include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water or using them in teas, tinctures, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
